options.why: Added $10$ to the top because $10$ was added to the bottom ($5\to 15$); equivalence multiplies, it does not add.
Wrote the scale factor $3$ as the numerator instead of multiplying $2$ by $3$.
Multiplied $2$ by the denominator $5$ rather than by the scale factor $3$.
Added $6$ to the bottom because $6$ was added to the top ($3\to 9$); equivalence multiplies, not adds.
Multiplied $9 \times 3$ using the numerator instead of scaling the denominator $4$.
Wrote the scale factor $3$ as the denominator rather than $4 \times 3$.
Kept the original $2$ shaded parts without doubling them when each third was split in two.
Wrote shaded parts over the original number of thirds instead of over the new total of six parts.
Counted $3$ (the original number of parts) as shaded rather than the $4$ shaded sixths.
Subtracted $20$ from the top because $20$ was subtracted from the bottom ($25\to 5$); equivalence divides, not subtracts.
Wrote the divisor $5$ as the numerator instead of $40 \div 5$.
Subtracted $5$ from $40$ instead of dividing both parts by $5$.
Added $2$ to both parts; adding the same amount does not keep the fraction equal.
Added $3$ to both parts instead of multiplying both by the same number.
Added $5$ to both parts; equivalence multiplies both, it does not add.
Rewrote the denominator as $12$ but left the numerator unchanged at $6$.
Multiplied $\frac{6}{8}$ by $\frac{3}{3}$ to get $\frac{18}{24}$ and read $18$, instead of simplifying to $\frac{3}{4}$ first.
Simplified $\frac{6}{8}$ to $\frac{3}{4}$ and stopped, reading the denominator $4$ as the answer.
